checks.a_analysis: I examined the converted JSON for text that does not appear in the source PDF. The JSON includes AI-generated image alt text and table captions (e.g., 'Goal 1.1 Priorities'), which are permitted. The blockquotes used for highlighted text in the source (e.g., on pages 31, 36, and 38) are faithful to the original. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified.

I verified the contact information in the output against the source. The URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' found on page 41 of the PDF is correctly preserved in the output. No phone numbers, emails, or other URLs are present in the converted sections. No fabricated contact information was found.

The output JSON contains a placeholder stating '17 sections omitted for token budget'. Upon comparing the Table of Contents (pages 2-4) and the document body with the JSON sections, I confirmed that significant top-level sections and pages are missing. These include 'About the Council' (page 7), 'What is GIS?' (page 8), 'FY23-24 Council Highlights' (page 10), 'Accomplishments 2023-2024' (page 16), and 'Municipal Boundary Working Group' (page 32). This is a critical omission of more than half the document's content.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source contains full sections for 'About the Council' (p. 7), 'What is GIS?' (p. 8), 'FY23-24 Council Highlights' (p. 10), 'Accomplishments 2023-2024' (p. 16), and 'Municipal Boundary Working Group' (p. 32).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON contains a section with the text: '17 sections omitted for token budget' and skips from the Introduction to the Strategic Direction, and later skips the Municipal Boundary Working Group between the Seamless Parcels and Broadband sections.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 4 and 7, spanning various pages between 7 and 32

checks.a_analysis: I checked the row counts for the tables that were successfully converted. The 'Goal' tables (1.1 through 2.4) match the source row counts and content layout. Table 1 ('Required attributes for Seamless Parcels') on page 31 contains all 22 data rows found in the source. Table 2 ('Outreach presentations') on page 40 contains all 7 data rows found in the source. No rows were missing from the converted tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The sections that were included in the JSON (Summary, Introduction, Strategic Direction, Broadband, Priorities, and Appendix) appear to be full and faithful representations of the corresponding sections in the source PDF. The massive content gaps are treated as complete section omissions under check C3.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: I compared several passages from the source and output for wording changes. The conversion is highly accurate, even preserving typos from the original source (e.g., 'regiser of deeds' in Table 1 on page 31). Meaning and data points (such as the broadband metrics in Figure 26) are preserved accurately.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON follows the content flow of the source PDF. I checked for repeated paragraphs or sections, particularly in the sections containing tables and lists (Strategic Direction, Future Priorities, Appendix). No text blocks were found to be duplicated incorrectly.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent with the source document's organization. Major sections like 'Executive Summary', 'Introduction', 'Goal 1', 'Intergovernmental Collaboration...', and 'Appendix 1' are correctly identified as top-level or primary headings (H2/Section Titles). Subsections such as 'AddressNC' and 'Lidar Working Group' are appropriately nested as H3. No levels are skipped (e.g., H2 to H4).

The tables in the JSON output, particularly the complex layout boxes in the 'Strategic Direction' section (pages 14-16) and the 'Required attributes for Seamless Parcels' table (page 31), are faithfully reconstructed. The use of rowspans in the JSON correctly captures the visual spanning of note cells across multiple attribute rows in the source PDF (e.g., the note for 'Record Area' and 'Owner First Name' columns). Header rows are correctly identified.
The reading order in the JSON follows the logical flow of the source PDF, starting from the title page and proceeding through the executive summary and main report sections to the appendix.

Lists in the 'Future Priorities' and 'Appendix 1' sections are correctly identified and structured. The acronyms in the appendix are maintained as a single list corresponding to the bulleted list on pages 42 and 43 of the PDF.
The link provided in the 'Future Priorities' section (https://it.nc.gov/gicc) is correctly identified and matches the source PDF. No fabricated or broken links were detected in the reviewed sections.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully represents the visual organization of the source PDF. The information is presented in the same logical sequence, and the transition from a fixed-page layout to a responsive, single-page HTML format is handled correctly. Section headings, subheadings, and content blocks are appropriately ordered and visually distinct. The linearized presentation of images and text maintains the source's intent and narrative flow.

All tables and data-grid structures from the PDF are correctly converted into functional HTML tables. This includes the 'North Carolina Report Card' (Figure 6 on page 11), the various 'Goal' tables (pages 19-21), 'Required attributes for Seamless Parcels' (Table 1 on page 36), and 'Outreach presentations' (Table 2 on page 45). The tables maintain clear row/column alignment, headers are distinct, and all content remains perfectly legible and understandable in the HTML rendering.

Every meaningful image from the source PDF is present and properly rendered in the HTML output. This includes the organizational logos (NCGICC), photographs of council members and meetings (Figures 1, 5, 12, 13, 16), complex diagrams (Figures 2, 3), and numerous technical GIS screenshots and maps (Figures 4, 7-11, 14, 15, 17-32). All graphics are correctly positioned within their respective sections and contribute to the document's information as intended.

A thorough visual audit of the HTML output confirms there is no unintended duplication of body content. Paragraphs, lists, and tables appear exactly once in the document flow, matching the source material. While section titles and headings are correctly present in both the navigation sidebar (TOC) and the body of the document, this is standard accessible design and does not constitute content duplication.
